frontmatter.title: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC resetForm Use-After-Free Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
frontmatter.excerpt: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC has a use-after-free vulnerability in the resetForm method, enabling remote attackers to execute arbitrary code. User interaction, such as visiting a malicious page or opening a malicious file, is necessary for exploitation. (CVE-2023-21608)

frontmatter.title: Foxit PDF Reader Out-of-Bound Read Information Disclosure Vulnerability
frontmatter.excerpt: Foxit PDF Reader is vulnerable to an out-of-bound read flaw, allowing remote attackers to disclose sensitive information. User interaction, like visiting a malicious page or opening a malicious file, is needed for exploitation. This flaw, when combined with other vulnerabilities, can lead to arbitrary code execution. CVE ID is currently awaiting.

frontmatter.title: Foxit PDF Reader Choice Field Use-After-Free Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
frontmatter.excerpt: Foxit PDF Reader has a use-after-free vulnerability in its handling of Choice field objects, enabling remote attackers to execute arbitrary code. User interaction, such as visiting a malicious page or opening a malicious file, is necessary for exploitation. Note: This was identified as a bug collision by ZDI at the vendor's end.

frontmatter.title: Foxit PDF Reader Doc Object Use-After-Free Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
frontmatter.excerpt: Foxit PDF Reader is susceptible to a use-after-free flaw in the handling of Doc objects, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary code. For exploitation, user interaction is needed, such as visiting a malicious page or opening a malicious file. (CVE-2022-37385)

frontmatter.title: Foxit PDF Reader Doc Object Use-After-Free Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
frontmatter.excerpt: Foxit PDF Reader has a use-after-free vulnerability in its handling of Doc objects, enabling remote attackers to execute arbitrary code. User interaction, by visiting a malicious page or opening a malicious file, is necessary for exploitation. (CVE-2022-28672)

frontmatter.title: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC AcroForm Use-After-Free Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
frontmatter.excerpt: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC is vulnerable to a use-after-free flaw in AcroForms, enabling remote attackers to execute arbitrary code. User interaction, such as visiting a malicious page or opening a malicious file, is necessary for exploitation. (CVE-2022-27789)

frontmatter.title: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC Annotation Out-Of-Bounds Read Information Disclosure Vulnerability
frontmatter.excerpt: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC has an out-of-bounds read vulnerability in the handling of Annotation objects, allowing remote attackers to disclose sensitive information. User interaction, like visiting a malicious page or opening a malicious file, is needed for exploitation. This flaw can be combined with other vulnerabilities for arbitrary code execution. (CVE-2022-28249)

frontmatter.title: Foxit PDF Reader Doc Use-After-Free Information Disclosure Vulnerability
frontmatter.excerpt: Foxit PDF Reader is vulnerable to a use-after-free flaw in the handling of Doc objects, allowing remote attackers to disclose sensitive information. User interaction, such as visiting a malicious page or opening a malicious file, is required for exploitation. This vulnerability can be combined with other flaws to achieve arbitrary code execution. (CVE-2022-24368)

frontmatter.title: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC AcroForm Use-After-Free Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
frontmatter.excerpt: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC has a use-after-free vulnerability in AcroForm fields, allowing remote code execution with user interaction, such as visiting a malicious page or opening a malicious file. (CVE-2021-44701)

frontmatter.title: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC AcroForm XFA ANSI-Unicode Confusion Information Leak Vulnerability
frontmatter.excerpt: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC has an information leak vulnerability in the AcroForm module due to ANSI-Unicode confusion, allowing remote attackers to disclose sensitive data. User interaction, like visiting a malicious page or opening a malicious file, is needed for exploitation. This flaw can be combined with other vulnerabilities for arbitrary code execution. (CVE-2021-45067)
